params.text:
Welcome to Medication Awareness

Supporting people with their medicines is one of the most responsible things you will do in adult social care. A small mistake can cause real harm, so it is vital that everyone understands the principles of safe medicines support.

This course raises your awareness of how medicines are handled in care homes and in the community. It is based on the NICE guideline on managing medicines in care homes (SC1), guidance from the Care Quality Commission (CQC), and the safe-handling principles set out by the Royal Pharmaceutical Society and Skills for Care.

By the end you will be able to describe the care worker's role and its limits, the "rights" of medication, common routes and forms, how to read and record on a MAR chart, and how medicines are ordered, stored and disposed of. You will also understand controlled drugs, PRN medicines, covert administration, self-administration and how to report errors.

Important: this is awareness only

This course does not qualify you to administer medicines. Administering medicines to the people you support is a task that requires additional, employer-provided training and a formal competency assessment, where an assessor observes you and confirms you are safe to do it.

  • Never administer or handle medicines as a task until your employer has trained you and signed off your competency.
  • Your competency should be reviewed at least once a year, and sooner if a medicines incident happens.
  • Always work within your organisation's medicines policy and your job role.

If you are ever unsure, stop and ask a senior colleague, the pharmacist or the prescriber before acting.
